senses.definition: to attract the attention of a person

examples.x: As she turned back, a movement across the lawn caught her eye.
I was walking through the market when a beautiful dress caught my eye.
The vibrant colors of the artwork in the gallery really caught my eye.

senses.definition: to be able to hear even the quietest of sounds due to the environment being extremely silent

examples.x: I was expecting others to be here but now there's only the two of us and we can hear a pin drop .
Sarah said she could have heard a pin drop as the audience intently watched the actors on stage.
As the actor delivered the powerful monologue on stage, you could have heard a pin drop in the audience.
When the principal announced the new school policy, the entire assembly fell so silent you could hear a pin drop.
As the job interview began, the room was so quiet you could hear a pin drop, making the candidates even more nervous.

word_list.pronunciation: st…πÀà åt wÀà ånz stÀà åf

senses.definition: to confidently put one's best skills, abilities, or features on display in order to impress or show off

examples.x: After a year of training, he went up onto the stage to strut his stuff.
I saw you at the club last night, strutting your stuff on the dance floor!
I swear these dogs know they're being judged‚Äîlook how they strut their stuff!

senses.definition: to be eager to hear what a person wants to say

examples.x: I'm all ears - tell us what they had to say.
I'm all ears, please tell me what happened during your trip.
Whenever she starts talking about her childhood, I'm all ears.
I'm all ears if you have any tips on how to improve my cooking.

senses.definition: to give a thing one's full attention

examples.x: We were glued to the television watching the election results come in.
When I'm deeply focused on a creative project, I'm glued to my desk and can lose track of time.

senses.definition: the skill to notice details and make great observations

examples.x: For once, Beatrice's eagle eye failed to detect the tenderness in Timothy whenever he spoke to Topaz.
We need to get Sally's eagle eye on this manuscript because she'll be sure to spot any errors.
The detective's eagle eye spotted a small clue that led to a breakthrough in the case.
The coach's eagle eye for talent helped him discover a young athlete's potential early in their career.

senses.definition: to be careful to notice a thing or person

examples.x: We kept our eyes skinned for a sign that would tell us where to turn.
While you are checking the animals, keep your eyes open for any signs of disease .
While patrolling the premises, the security guard keeps his eyes peeled for any unusual activity.
When looking for your lost keys, make sure to keep your eyes peeled for any shiny or metallic objects.

senses.definition: to make an effort to stay well informed on the developments or changes of a situation

examples.x: John had his ear to the ground, hoping to find out about new ideas in computers.
In the world of business, successful entrepreneurs keep their ear to the ground, tracking market trends and consumer preferences.
As a journalist, it's crucial to have your ear close to the ground, constantly seeking out stories and sources to report the latest news.
To excel in the tech industry, you need to have your ear to the ground, staying updated on the latest advancements and developments.

senses.definition: to listen to a person or thing in an attentive or sympathetic way

examples.x: Even if you disagree with someone, you should still lend them your ear and try to understand their point of view.
I wasn't solving anything just by lending her my ear.
The boss asked us all to lend him our ears for an hour this afternoon.

word_list.pronunciation: pÀà…úÀêk p…πÀà…™k Àå åp wÀà ånz Àà…™…πz

senses.definition: to hear something interesting and start to listen to it carefully

examples.x: He seemed to like me being there and always perked his ears up when I started reading to him.
He used obscene words that pricked my ears.
I can't help but prick up my ears if I hear someone say my name‚Äîeven if they aren't talking about me.
So, if there's a simple way to make your business more profitable, you should perk your ears.

senses.definition: to pay attention to something that is being said

examples.x: I'm just pinning back my ears because I'm not smart enough to understand the science behind the tech.
The professor told the students to pin back their ears, as he was about to reveal the key concepts for the upcoming exam.
During the halftime talk, the coach urged the players to pin back their ears and follow the new strategy carefully.

senses.definition: to attract the attention of other people, often in a way that causes other people or things less noticeable

examples.x: I really don't want our production issues to take the stage during the investors' meeting.
My wedding was supposed to be my special day, but my sister and her stupid boyfriend took the stage when he proposed to her at the wedding reception.
The rockstar took the stage and started playing his guitar, electrifying the crowd with his music.
The CEO took the stage at the annual conference and delivered a keynote speech that inspired the audience.

word_list.pronunciation: kÀàiÀêp me…™ntÀàe…™n …ê lÀào ä p…πÀào äfa…™l

senses.definition: to behave in a way that does not draw attention to one

examples.x: Hard to believe that Jefferson kept a low profile despite being all drunk.
He's been in a little trouble recently so he's trying to keep a low profile.
Maintaining a low profile has never been Maggie's strongest suit.
The company kept a low profile and avoided advertising their new product to avoid attracting competition.

word_list.pronunciation: p…πÀà…™k Àå åp wÀà ånz Àà…™…πz

senses.definition: to begin listening eagerly and intentively

examples.x: The children pricked up their ears when they heard the word 'ice-cream'.
As soon as the boss mentioned possible promotions, the employees pricked up their ears, hoping to hear more details.
During the dinner party, I pricked up my ears when I overheard a discussion about a topic I'm passionate about.
In a business meeting, it's important to prick up your ears to understand the client's needs and concerns.
